negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Capability value 7 no longer remains unknown in the audited port; it decodes as ImageCall.
related_but_insufficient_code: Capability::try_from(6) returns Dkms and Dkms is non-executor, but value 7 is assigned to ImageCall and is an executor capability.
Registry-reader tests assert Capability::try_from(7) == Ok(Capability::ImageCall), contradicting the invariant requirement that value 7 remain unknown.

notes: The DKMS portion is present, but the invariant also requires 7 to reject and the current multimodal capability layout contradicts that.
rationale: The primary diff adds Dkms = 6 to the Capability enum, maps byte value 6 to it in TryFrom<u8>, and updates tests to assert Dkms is not an executor capability while 7 is still rejected. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Capability::try_from(6) must return Capability::Dkms, Capability::try_from(7) must remain unknown, and Capability::Dkms must not be classified as an executor capability.
suggested_falsification_tests: Unit-test the registry reader capability conversion and classification: bytes 0-6 map to known capabilities with 6 as Dkms, byte 7 errors, and is_executor_capability returns false for Dkms while remaining true for executor capabilities.
summary: DKMS is non-executor, but value 7 is not unknown.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Unit-test the registry reader capability conversion and classification: bytes 0-6 map to known capabilities with 6 as Dkms, byte 7 errors, and is_executor_capability returns false for Dkms while remaining true for executor capabilities.
title: DKMS capability decodes as non-executor
